reasoning: The resolution criteria depend on the 3-day domestic opening weekend in 2025. 'A Minecraft Movie' had an opening weekend of $162.7M in April 2025. I have exhaustively checked its strongest competitors. Zootopia 2 opened with $96.8M for its 3-day weekend and $156M for its 5-day holiday total. Superman (2025) opened to $122M. Wicked: For Good opened to $150M. Lilo & Stitch opened to $146M. Avatar: Fire and Ash, released in late December, opened to just $88M. These figures systematically confirm 'A Minecraft Movie' maintained its number 1 standing throughout the entire year, with no movie crossing its $162.7M 3-day mark. The market previously priced this at 96% just prior to the Avatar 3 release. Given Avatar 3 underperformed compared to Minecraft, the resolution is effectively guaranteed to be YES, absent some extremely unlikely error from The Numbers or a retroactive adjustment of an unexpected magnitude. I estimate 98% to reflect this overwhelming evidence while leaving a tiny sliver for data anomalies.
